segments.text: And Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,
Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle him in his talk.
And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying,
Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man: for thou regardest not the person of men.
segments.speaker: Pharisees' Disciples and Herodians

segments.text: Tell us therefore, What thinkest thou? Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar, or not?
segments.speaker: Pharisees' Disciples and Herodians

segments.text: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said,
Cæsar's
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The same day came to him the Sadducees, which say that there is no resurrection, and asked him,
Master, Moses said, If a man die, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
segments.speaker: Sadducees

segments.text: Jesus answered and said unto them,
Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when the Pharisees had heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together.
Then one of them, which was a lawyer, asked him a question, tempting him, and saying,
Master, which is the great commandment in the law?
segments.speaker: Lawyer

segments.text: Jesus said unto him,
Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
They say unto him, The Son of David.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: And no man was able to answer him a word, neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.
